title: Knightfall Trilogy

{{Quote|Lucas said they weren't happy with the way the story was going, so rather than salvaging what had been done, they canned it.|Terese Nielsen|<ref name="knightfall" />}}
The '''''Knightfall'' Trilogy''' was a three-book cycle set to be published in [[2001]] as part of [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series. Author [[Michael Jan Friedman]] was contracted by [[Del Rey]] Books and [[Lucasfilm]] to write the trilogy, which would have contained three volumes: [[The New Jedi Order: Knightfall I: Jedi Storm|''Jedi Storm'']], [[The New Jedi Order: Knightfall II: Jedi Fire|''Jedi Fire'']], and [[The New Jedi Order: Knightfall III: Jedi Blood|''Jedi Blood'']]. ''Knightfall'' was going to star the [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Danni Quee]] and the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jorallen]], both of whom were caught up in the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and [[New Jedi Order]]'s ongoing [[Yuuzhan Vong War|war]] with the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extragalactic]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[True Way|religious]] zealots known as the [[Yuuzhan Vong]].

After Friedman finished writing ''Jedi Storm'', however, Del Rey and Lucasfilm Ltd. decided to cancel the trilogy. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Anakin Solo]] was slated to [[Death/Legends|die]] in the upcoming hardcover novel [[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star|''Star by Star'']], and ''The New Jedi Order''{{'s}} planning team wanted the books leading into ''Star by Star'' to focus on his character. [[Greg Keyes]] was thus contracted to write the [[Edge of Victory Duology|''Edge of Victory'' Duology]], a pair of Solo-centric novels that replaced ''Knightfall''. Although ''Jedi Storm'' had been completed, neither it nor either of its successors were ever published.

==Plot summary==
[[File:Jedi_Fire_01.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Preliminary cover art of ''Jedi Fire'']]
The ''Knightfall'' Trilogy was a planned three-book cycle within ''[[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|The New Jedi Order]]''. Its first novel, [[The New Jedi Order: Knightfall I: Jedi Storm|''Jedi Storm'']], was completed but unpublished, and it explores the ramifications of a declaration made by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] at the end of the novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point|Balance Point]]''.<ref name="knightfall" /><ref name="lostworlds">{{WebCite|url=http://home.earthlink.net/~kcfurman/books.html|text=The Novels & Other Books|work=Earthlink.net|archivedate=20071023023304}}</ref> Lah claimed that as-yet-[[Yuuzhan Vong War|unconquered]] [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] would be spared the full wrath of the invaders' war machine if they brought him [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]],<ref name="bp" /> and ''Jedi Storm'' features [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] being [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] by citizens of those worlds and by a [[Unidentified Yuuzhan Vong priestess|Yuuzhan Vong priestess]].<ref name="knightfall" /><ref name="lostworlds" />

[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]],<ref name="knightfall" /><ref name="lostworlds" /> three primary characters from the [[Original trilogy|original ''Star Wars'' trilogy]],<ref name="eod">''[[Empire of Dreams: The Story of the Star Wars Trilogy]]''</ref> play a role in the book, helping to hide the hunted [[New Jedi Order|Jedi]]<ref name="knightfall" /><ref name="lostworlds" /> from such groups as the [[Peace Brigade]].<ref name="Reader's Companion" /> The [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] and former Yuuzhan Vong [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] [[Danni Quee]] hopes to avoid the invaders, but she becomes wrapped up in an investigation into missing Jedi after meeting the mysterious Jedi Knight [[Jorallen]]. As the two move deep into enemy territory, Quee begins to discover secrets about Jorallen, as well.<ref name="knightfall" /><ref name="lostworlds" />

The next two novels in the trilogy, [[The New Jedi Order: Knightfall II: Jedi Fire|''Jedi Fire'']] and [[The New Jedi Order: Knightfall III: Jedi Blood|''Jedi Blood'']], were never completed, but they were set to tell the story of Quee and Jorallen's investigation leading them to the priestess and defeating her. Meanwhile, Jedi across the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] would be [[Death/Legends|slaughtered]] in an effort to appease the Yuuzhan Vong.<ref name="knightfall" /><ref name="lostworlds" />

When the planning team of ''The New Jedi Order'' decided to cancel the ''Knightfall'' Trilogy and focus more heavily on the character of [[Anakin Solo]], a storyline involving the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentience]] in the wake of the Yuuzhan Vong's [[Anti-droid sentiment/Legends|harsh treatment]] of droids was ultimately dropped. The story had begun in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial|Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]'', the fourth book of the series.<ref name="knightfall" /><ref name="Wrapping Up" />

==Development==
{{Quote|My other favorite is the first one I did for Del Rey, called Knightfall. This was going to be the first of a 3-part series for the 'New Jedi Order.'|Terese Nielsen|<ref name="Daydream">{{WebCite|url=http://daydream-graphics.com/artists/nielsen/tnmain.shtml|text=Terese Nielsen|archivedate=20001204155800}}</ref>}}
In [[1999]], [[Del Rey]] books and [[Lucasfilm]] began publishing ''The New Jedi Order'', a multi-authored series of ''[[Star Wars]]'' novels that told the story of an alien invasion. Within the series, an [[Extragalactic/Legends|extragalactic]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of religious zealots called the Yuuzhan Vong invaded the galaxy, but their [[Blood/Legends|bloodthirsty]] efforts were repulsed by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and New Jedi Order.<ref name="vp">''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]''</ref><ref name="onslaught">''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]''</ref> The series was set to be a chronological storyline covering a five-[[Standard year/Legends|year]] span of [[Timeline of galactic history/Legends|galactic history]] and would see one hardcover novel published every year that chronicled a major event of the war. Additionally, several paperback books would be published throughout each year to add to the story and fill in any gaps.<ref name="unjoh interviews: Liang">{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/books/unjoh/interviews/index.html#liang|text=Kuo-Yu Liang Interview|archivedate=20190730225123}}</ref> By the end of [[2000]], two hardcovers and four paperbacks had been published.<ref name="vp" /><ref name="onslaught" /><ref name="ruin">''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide II: Ruin]]''</ref><ref name="ht">''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]''</ref><ref name="je">''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]''</ref><ref name="bp">''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]''</ref>

[[File:Michaeljanfriedman.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Michael Jan Friedman, the author of ''Knightfall'']]
[[Michael Jan Friedman]], a veteran author of [[Memoryalpha:Star Trek|''Star Trek'']] novels,<ref name="trek">{{OfficialSite|url=http://www.michaeljanfriedman.net/star-trek|text=Michael Jan Friedman's official website|archivedate=20171009221250}}</ref> was contracted in 2000 to write a trilogy of books to follow ''Balance Point'', the hardcover that was slated to cap off that year. Although no announcement of the trilogy appeared on [[StarWars.com]], news began to reach several third-party websites when cover artist [[Terese Nielsen]] published a rough sketch of the cover of the trilogy's first volume on her website in [[June]] 2000. She also revealed that it had been christened the "''Knightfall'' Trilogy."<ref name="knightfall" />

Over the next several months, third-party websites and the [[Random House]] online catalog revealed the names of the trilogy's individual volumes: ''Jedi Storm'', ''Jedi Fire'', and ''Jedi Blood''. In [[August]], an entry on ''Jedi Storm'' appeared in [[Wikipedia:Publishers Weekly|''Publishers Weekly'']]. The entry revealed that it would feature Danni Quee,<ref name="knightfall" /> who had been introduced in [[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime|''Vector Prime'']], the opening novel of ''The New Jedi Order'';<ref name="vp" /> as well as the Jedi Knight Jorallen, a new character. Friedman completed writing ''Jedi Storm'' at some point during 2000.<ref name="knightfall" /> All three entries in ''Knightfall'' were to be paperbacks.<ref name="Reader's Companion">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref>

===Cancellation===
{{Quote|Mike Friedman had completed only JEDI STORM, and believe me, none of us felt good discarding even that much work. But on the whole, it was agreed that this was the best thing for the overall Star Wars publishing program.|Shelly Shapiro|<ref name="knightfall" />}}
Around the time of ''Balance Point''{{'s}} publication, Del Rey and Lucasfilm Ltd. decided to shift a large part of ''The New Jedi Order''{{'s}} focus, which in turn necessitated the cancellation of the ''Knightfall'' Trilogy.<ref name="knightfall" /> The character of Anakin Solo was slated to die in [[2001]]'s hardcover, [[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star|''Star by Star'']], and the planning team wanted to spotlight him prior to his demise.<ref name="Wrapping Up">{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/books/unjoh/interviews/lucenotos.html|text=Wrapping It All Up - An Interview with James Luceno|archivedate=20200201043532}}</ref> [[Greg Keyes]], who had been set to pen a single ''New Jedi Order'' novel,<ref name="tfn">{{WebCite|url=http://theforce.net/books/story/knightfall_trilogy_cancelled_74835.asp|text=Knightfall Trilogy Cancelled?|work=TheForce.Net|archivedate=20180922125320}}</ref> was thus contracted to write the [[Edge of Victory Duology|''Edge of Victory'' Duology]], a pair of books that would replace ''Knightfall'' and focus heavily on Solo.<ref name="conquest">''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]''</ref><ref name="rebirth">''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory II: Rebirth]]''</ref> No further ''New Jedi Order'' novels were published between ''Edge of Victory'' and ''Star by Star'', which allowed Del Rey to highlight several novels set during the [[Rise of the Empire era|prequel era]] in their 2001 publishing schedule.<ref name="knightfall" /> [[Kathy Tyers]], the author of ''Balance Point'', also indicated that the cancellation was done in part to allow more attention to be paid to ''The New Jedi Order''{{'s}} hardcovers.<ref name="tyers6">{{JCFCite|url=posts/1231528|board=Literature|thread=Discuss BP with Kathy -- Spoilers Welcome|user=[[Kathy Tyers|Shmi52]]|date=2000-11-01|time=7:06 AM|archivedate=20140116225534}}</ref>

[[File:Conquestanakin.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Anakin Solo, the largest beneficiary of ''Knightfall'{{'s}} cancellation]]
By [[October]] 2000, all entries for ''Knightfall'' had been removed from the Random House catalog and been replaced by entries for Keyes's duology. Confirmation that the trilogy had been canceled came at the end of the month from Tyers<ref name="tyers3">{{JCFCite|url=posts/1227891|board=Literature|thread=Discuss BP with Kathy -- Spoilers Welcome|user=[[Kathy Tyers|Shmi52]]|date=2000-10-31|time=3:45 PM|archivedate=20150928013751}}</ref> and again several days later from Nielsen, who stated on her website that Lucasfilm Ltd. had been unhappy with the direction of the trilogy and had axed it. She also posted black-and-white cover art of ''Jedi Fire'' on her site. Later in the month, StarWars.com officially announced the ''Edge of Victory'' Duology, and [[Shelly Shapiro]] of Del Rey released a statement regarding ''Knightfall''{{'s}} cancellation. She regretted discarding a completed novel, but she was confident that the decision would produce a positive result for ''Star Wars'' publishing.<ref name="knightfall" />

==Continuity==
{{Quote|The Jedi you trained and inspired are hunted and killed throughout the galaxy, and you ‘understand’ it the way you might an equation? Your blood doesn’t burn to do something about it?|The Jedi [[Kyp Durron]], in ''Edge of Victory I: Conquest''|<ref name="conquest" />}}
Although the ''Knightfall'' Trilogy never saw the light of day,<ref name="knightfall" /> some of the storylines it would have included did. The ''Edge of Victory'' Duology dealt with public response to Tsavong Lah's declaration, and it featured a number of Jedi being killed by Yuuzhan Vong sympathizers across the galaxy. Meanwhile, Han and Leia Organa Solo began creating a [[Great River]] where Jedi could take refuge.<ref name="conquest" /><ref name="rebirth" /> Danni Quee reappeared in ''Star by Star'' and went on to play a major role in later books of the series.<ref name="fh1">''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic I: Remnant]]''</ref><ref name="fh2">''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]''</ref><ref name="reunion">''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic III: Reunion]]''</ref> The subplot involving C-3PO and droid sentience was only briefly touched on again, however.<ref name="rs">''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand]]''</ref><ref name="tuf">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref>
